$comment: Hand-maintained structured layer over docs/*.md. See CONTENT.md for how and when to edit this file. decisionPhases: which phases each decision affects (powers cross-links). openDecisions: the ⚠️ tracker on the dashboard — when you decide one, set status to 'decided' and write the outcome in resolution; also update the guide text itself. flags: manual flagged-for-review notes (auto-detected ones are generated at build). materialsPhaseMap: master-list row label → phase(s), drives the per-phase shopping lists.

openDecisions.title: Grill: Coyote SL 30" vs 36" — and LP vs NG
openDecisions.deadlineText: Before framing the grill bay (Phase 1, Step 1.6)
openDecisions.method: Cardboard mockup at 25½" vs 32½" cutout widths on the real sill once it's down.

openDecisions.title: Backsplash tile — four comps; Sea Glass Sage recommended
openDecisions.deadlineText: Before the counter is templated (Phase 6); practically before the Phase 4 tile order if you want one combined order
openDecisions.method: Pick from the four rendered comps — Sea Glass Sage is the standing recommendation next to the red Big Joe.

openDecisions.title: Door hinges: clamp-on concealed (Euro opening preferred) vs piano
openDecisions.deadlineText: Phase 5, before tiling the door faces
openDecisions.method: Shortlist clamp-on concealed hinges for aluminum-frame doors; piano hinge is the sag-proof fallback. Phase 1 hinge backing supports either.

openDecisions.title: Sewer + gas tie-in timing / permit
openDecisions.deadlineText: No build deadline — the jug and capped stubs bridge it
openDecisions.method: When it happens, permitted is cheap insurance relative to the trench labor. Confirm the path with San Diego DSD.

flags.summary: Coyote SL conversion kit costs ~$320 at the manufacturer — the guide budgets ~$60–100 for the LP→NG swap.
flags.details: Owner-verified July 2026 on coyotegrill.com: SL-series kits exist BOTH directions — LP→NG (C3LP2NG) and NG→LP (C3NG2LP) — at roughly $320 each, so budget ~$250 more than the guide's Phase −1 fuel note and D11 estimate (installer hour still applies). Both-way availability also de-risks the LP-first strategy: the fuel choice is reversible for the kit price.
